one_liner: Freshdesk is a customer support/helpdesk platform offering a broad, well-documented REST API (v2) secured via HTTP Basic Auth with per-agent API keys, self-serve during trial/paid plans, with several community MCP servers already available.

auth_methods.notes: HTTP Basic Auth using personal API key as username and any string (e.g. 'X') as password. Key found in agent profile settings.

self_serve_notes: 14-day free trial gives full API access (50 calls/min). The permanent Free Program (up to 2 agents) sets API rate limit to zero, effectively disabling API access. Continued API use requires a paid plan.

api_surface.notes: REST API v2 covering tickets, contacts, companies, conversations, agents, groups, knowledge base articles; JSON in/out, supports CORS, standard HTTP verbs. Also has a JS SDK wrapper.

main_blocker: API access is disabled on the permanent Free plan (0 calls/min); a paid plan or active trial is required for programmatic access, and self-serve API key generation requires an agent account with sufficient permissions.

evidence.supports: Freshdesk REST API v2 documentation, Basic Auth via API key
JS SDK and Basic Auth details, domain + api_key usage
Pricing plans including free trial and Free Program
Free Program API rate limit set to zero calls per minute
REST API architecture, resources, rate limits, pagination details

flags: Free plan disables API access entirely (0 calls/min)
Multiple unofficial third-party MCP servers exist; no single official Freshworks-maintained MCP is GA (Enterprise EAP only)
Rate limits vary significantly by plan tier
